proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e=1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e=0`.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation=0`.

analyses.code: Welch two-group contrast: mean(dependency_BRAF | braf_v600e=1) - mean(dependency_BRAF | braf_v600e=0).
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1` lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8047 versus -0.2495 in `braf_v600e=0` lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-group contrast: mean(dependency_EGFR | egfr_amplification=1) - mean(dependency_EGFR | egfr_amplification=0).
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6010 versus -0.2524 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-group contrast: mean(dependency_ERBB2 | erbb2_amplification=1) - mean(dependency_ERBB2 | erbb2_amplification=0).
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.6856 versus -0.2535 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-group contrast: mean(dependency_KRAS | kras_mutation=1) - mean(dependency_KRAS | kras_mutation=0).
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5594 versus -0.2520 in wild-type lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent `lineage=skin` association with more negative `dependency_BRAF` is explained by `braf_v600e` and will not remain independently negative after adjustment.
The apparent `lineage=brain` association with more negative `dependency_EGFR` is explained by `egfr_amplification` and will not remain independently negative after adjustment.
Among `kras_mutation=1` lines, `lineage=pancreatic` does not further strengthen `dependency_KRAS`; the interaction between `kras_mutation` and `lineage=pancreatic` should be near zero.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss=1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss=0`, independent of growth and Cas9 activity.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + I(lineage == 'skin').
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for `braf_v600e`, `lineage=skin` was not independently associated with more negative `dependency_BRAF` (beta=-0.0021).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + I(lineage == 'brain').
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for `egfr_amplification`, `lineage=brain` was not independently associated with more negative `dependency_EGFR` (beta=0.0029).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + I(lineage == 'pancreatic') + I(lineage == 'colorectal') + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + kras_mutation:I(lineage == 'pancreatic').
analyses.result_summary: The `kras_mutation` main effect remained strongly negative, while the `kras_mutation` by pancreatic-lineage interaction was essentially zero (beta=-0.0004).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RIT1` (beta=-0.0953; unadjusted means -0.3527 versus -0.2572).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`>=0.964`, the upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than lower-growth lines.
Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`>=0.964`, the upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than lower-growth lines.
Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`>=0.964`) and high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (`>=5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than the complement.

analyses.code: Upper-quartile subgroup contrast for growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 versus -0.3928 in lower-growth lines.

analyses.code: Upper-quartile subgroup contrast for growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 versus -0.2447 in lower-growth lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup contrast: growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964 and rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm>=5.669.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth/MYC-high lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 versus -0.3297 in the complement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `copy_number_8q24` (`>=0.428`, the upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than lines below that threshold.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than lines with `myc_amplification=0`.
Higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` on the dependency-score scale.

analyses.code: Upper-quartile subgroup contrast for copy_number_8q24.
analyses.result_summary: High-8q24 lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3440 versus -0.2452 in lower-8q24 lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-group contrast for myc_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 versus -0.2523 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Simple linear slope: dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` became more negative as `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` increased; slope was -0.0161 dependency units per log2 TPM.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `lineage='colorectal'` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation=0`.
Cell lines with high `wnt_activity_score` (`>=0.819`, the upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-WNT lines.
Cell lines with high `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` (`>=3.559`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-ASCL2 lines.

analyses.code: Welch contrast: lineage == 'colorectal' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 versus -0.2524 in non-colorectal lines.

analyses.code: Welch contrast: apc_mutation == 1 versus apc_mutation == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation=1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 versus -0.2526 in `apc_mutation=0` lines.

analyses.code: Upper-quartile subgroup contrast for wnt_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: High-WNT lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.4694 versus -0.2526 in lower-WNT lines.

analyses.code: Upper-quartile subgroup contrast for rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm.
analyses.result_summary: High-ASCL2 lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.3880 versus -0.2797 in lower-ASCL2 lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `dependency_KIF18A` is concentrated in the complete subgroup `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score` (`>=0.819`), and `smad4_loss=0`; this subgroup has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` than all other cell lines.

Within `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, and high `wnt_activity_score` (`>=0.819`) cell lines, `smad4_loss=0` enables the KIF18A dependency and `smad4_loss=1` suppresses it, producing less negative `dependency_KIF18A`.

Adding high `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` (`>=3.559`) to the colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT/SMAD4-intact subgroup further enriches for more negative `dependency_KIF18A`, but the four-predicate subgroup remains strongly dependent without requiring ASCL2.

analyses.code: Four-predicate subgroup contrast: lineage == 'colorectal' and apc_mutation == 1 and wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 and smad4_loss == 0 versus all other lines.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high/SMAD4-intact subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 (n=2571) versus -0.2530 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Adjusted indicator model including the four component predicates and the complete subgroup indicator.
analyses.result_summary: The complete subgroup indicator remained strongly negative after accounting for its component predicates (beta=-1.0335).

analyses.code: Within colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high lines, contrast smad4_loss == 0 versus smad4_loss == 1.
analyses.result_summary: Within the colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high context, `smad4_loss=0` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990, whereas `smad4_loss=1` lines had mean -0.2557.

analyses.code: Five-predicate subgroup contrast adding rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559 to the colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high/SMAD4-intact subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: The ASCL2-high enriched subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.3320 (n=1511) versus -0.2749 in the complement, slightly stronger than the four-predicate subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` is independently associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` after adjusting for `myc_amplification`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, and `cas9_activity_score`.
Higher `copy_number_8q24` is independently associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` after adjusting for `myc_amplification`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is independently associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` after adjusting for `cas9_activity_score`, `copy_number_8q24`, and `myc_amplification`.

Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and higher `cas9_activity_score` are associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10`, suggesting broad screen or proliferation sensitivity rather than a sharply defined molecular subgroup.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + myc_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_DHX9` (beta=-0.0160).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_RPL11 ~ copy_number_8q24 + myc_amplification + rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: `copy_number_8q24` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RPL11` (beta=-0.1041).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_POLR2A ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + copy_number_8q24 + myc_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` retained a strong negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_POLR2A` (beta=-0.3053).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: dependency_TMED10 ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + nf1_loss + copy_number_8q24.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` was strongly negative for `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.1010), and `cas9_activity_score` was also negative (beta=-0.1035, p=1.78e-15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_BRAF` will identify `braf_v600e=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_BRAF`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_EGFR` will identify `egfr_amplification=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_EGFR`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_ERBB2` will identify `erbb2_amplification=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_ERBB2`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KRAS` will identify `kras_mutation=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_KRAS`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RIT1` will identify `nf1_loss=1` as the dominant molecular subgroup with more negative `dependency_RIT1`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KIF18A` will identify `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score`, and `smad4_loss=0` as the strongest complete dependency concentration.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_TMED10` will find high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` as the strongest broad subgroup, with only smaller molecular-feature effects.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_DHX9` will identify high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, especially with high growth, as the strongest subgroup with more negative `dependency_DHX9`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_POLR2A` will identify high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_POLR2A`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RPL11` will identify high `copy_number_8q24`, especially overlapping `myc_amplification=1`, as the strongest subgroup with more negative `dependency_RPL11`.

analyses.code: Systematic single-feature, pairwise, and small-subgroup scan over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The dominant BRAF subgroup was `braf_v600e=1` (mean -0.8047 versus -0.2495); added predicates did not materially improve the biological definition.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The dominant EGFR subgroup was `egfr_amplification=1` (mean -0.6010 versus -0.2524).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The dominant ERBB2 subgroup was `erbb2_amplification=1` (mean -0.6856 versus -0.2535).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The dominant KRAS subgroup was `kras_mutation=1` (mean -0.5594 versus -0.2520).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest molecular RIT1 subgroup was `nf1_loss=1` (mean -0.3527 versus -0.2572).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan followed by explicit complete subgroup and suppressor-predicate tests.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest KIF18A subgroup was `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0` (mean -1.2990 versus -0.2530).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted TMED10 model.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest broad TMED10 pattern was high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (mean -0.2816 versus -0.2447); `nf1_loss` was smaller after adjustment (beta=-0.0134).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted DHX9 model.
analyses.result_summary: High `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` lines had more negative `dependency_DHX9` (mean -0.3551 versus -0.3265), and the high-growth/MYC-high subgroup had effect -0.0595.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted POLR2A model.
analyses.result_summary: High `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` lines had more negative `dependency_POLR2A` (mean -0.4972 versus -0.3928).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted RPL11 model.
analyses.result_summary: High `copy_number_8q24` with `myc_amplification=1` had more negative `dependency_RPL11` (mean -0.3647 versus -0.2524), but adjusted modeling attributed the independent effect to continuous `copy_number_8q24`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `myc_amplification=1` will not add an independent negative `dependency_RPL11` effect after adjusting for continuous `copy_number_8q24`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.
`myc_amplification=1` will not add an independent negative `dependency_DHX9` effect after adjusting for continuous `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, and `cas9_activity_score`.
The weak raw associations between `dependency_POLR2A` and `copy_number_8q24` or `myc_amplification` will disappear after adjustment for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
`nf1_loss=1` has at most a small additional association with more negative `dependency_TMED10` after adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `copy_number_8q24`.

analyses.code: Coefficient for myc_amplification in adjusted RPL11 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification` was not independently negative for `dependency_RPL11` after continuous 8q24 copy number and MYC expression were included (beta=0.0089).

analyses.code: Coefficient for myc_amplification in adjusted DHX9 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification` was not independently negative for `dependency_DHX9` once continuous MYC expression was modeled (beta=0.0028).

analyses.code: Coefficients for copy_number_8q24 and myc_amplification in adjusted POLR2A OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: In the adjusted `dependency_POLR2A` model, `copy_number_8q24` was not significant (beta=-0.0038), and `myc_amplification` was not significant (beta=0.0084).

analyses.code: Coefficient for nf1_loss in adjusted TMED10 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss` had a small but significant negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.0134), much smaller than the growth and Cas9 effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final strongest biologically specific subgroup in the dataset is the `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup defined by `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0`, with high `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` further enriching but not defining the required subgroup.

Across the screen, higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` produces broadly more negative dependency scores for panessential-like outcomes including `dependency_POLR2A`, `dependency_TMED10`, and `dependency_DHX9`.

The final dependency-specific subgroup map is: BRAF by `braf_v600e=1`, EGFR by `egfr_amplification=1`, ERBB2 by `erbb2_amplification=1`, KRAS by `kras_mutation=1`, RIT1 by `nf1_loss=1`, KIF18A by colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT/SMAD4-intact status, TMED10 and POLR2A by high growth, DHX9 by high MYC expression with high growth, and RPL11 by high `copy_number_8q24`.

analyses.code: Compare signed subgroup effects from final systematic search across all dependency outcomes.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high/SMAD4-intact KIF18A subgroup had the largest negative complete-subgroup effect observed (mean difference=-1.0460), larger than the canonical driver effects.

analyses.code: Five-predicate ASCL2 enrichment check within the KIF18A subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Adding `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm>=3.559` sharpened the KIF18A effect to -1.0572, but the four-predicate subgroup was already strongly dependent.

analyses.code: Adjusted growth coefficients from OLS models for POLR2A, TMED10, and DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: Growth had negative adjusted coefficients for `dependency_POLR2A` (beta=-0.3053), `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.1010), and `dependency_DHX9` (beta approximately -0.1069), supporting a broad proliferation/screen-quality axis.

analyses.code: Synthesis of per-outcome systematic heterogeneity search results.
analyses.result_summary: The per-outcome search recovered expected direct oncogene dependencies, an NF1/RIT1 relationship, a buried colorectal/APC/WNT/SMAD4-intact KIF18A interaction, growth-linked TMED10 and POLR2A patterns, MYC-linked DHX9, and 8q24-linked RPL11.
